checks.evidence: The pricing page (pumble.com/pricing) positions Pumble as a business communication tool with channels, messages, threads, video/voice calls, file sharing, and integrations — consistent with 'Team communication and collaboration platform (Slack/Microsoft Teams alternative)'.

Rendered pages confirm three methods: (1) API key auth via the API Addon (ApiKey header, shown in Swagger docs and API keys help page); (2) OAuth2 flow (SDK docs at cake-com.github.io/pumble-node-sdk/authorization.html explicitly state 'Pumble uses OAuth2' with clientId/clientSecret/code flow); (3) Bearer tokens (MCP server page shows 'token' and 'x-app-token' headers, SDK docs describe accessToken/botToken as bearer tokens).

Pricing page FREE plan includes 'Up to 3 apps & integrations'. API keys help page states 'Available on all plans'. Sign-up is self-serve (prominent Sign Up button, no paywall). Self-serve free credential generation is supported.

Swagger docs (pumble-api-keys.addons.marketplace.cake.com/api-docs) show a REST API with endpoints for Channels, Messages, Scheduled Messages, Users. Incoming webhooks page documents webhook posting. MCP server page documents a separate MCP protocol. Breadth is broad — multiple API surfaces and many endpoints.

MCP help page (pumble.com/help/integrations/automation-workflow-integrations/how-to-use-the-pumble-mcp-server) documents an official MCP server at 'https://mcp.pumble.com/mcp' with 19 tools, instructions for Claude CLI, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI. Explicitly called 'Pumble MCP server'.

All rendered pages support the verdict: free self-serve API (API key auth, 3 apps on free plan), comprehensive REST API, official MCP server, webhooks, and SDK are all documented and available. No material blockers observed.
